name: Caper Egg Salad in Pasta Shells
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a large bowl with about 2 quarts cold water; add oil and set aside.
2. In a 5 to 6 quart pan, bring about 3 quarts water to a boil over high heat. Add pasta; let water return to a boil and cook, uncovered, until barely tender to bite (about 10 minutes). Or cook according to package directions. Remove pasta shells individually to avoid tearing.
3. Immerse the shells in cold water until completely cooled; drain well again.
4. Pat shells dry with paper towels.
5. Prepare Caper Egg Salad: Combine sour cream, mustard, capers, celery, and green onion; mix until well blended. Peel and chop the hard-boiled eggs; add to the sour cream mixture and mix lightly until well blended. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Fill each shell with salad. Arrange 1 or 2 lettuce leaves, if desired, on individual dinner plates. Set 4 or 5 shells on each plate and garnish with celery leaves, if desired. If made ahead, cover and refrigerate for up to 4 hours. Makes 4 servings.
